title: は (topic marker)
meaning: Marks the topic of the sentence — 'as for X'.

examples.reading: わたしはがくせいです。

title: が (subject marker)
meaning: Marks the grammatical subject, often new information.

title: を (object marker)
meaning: Marks the direct object of a verb.

title: に (to / at / in)
meaning: Indicates a destination, time, or location of existence.

title: で (by means of / at)
meaning: Marks the location of an action or the means used.

title: の (possessive / linking)
meaning: Links two nouns; shows possession or attribute.

title: か (question marker)
meaning: Turns a statement into a question.

title: ～ました / ～ませんでした (polite past)
meaning: Polite past affirmative and negative of verbs.
structure: [Verb-stem] ました / ませんでした

title: ～ません (polite negative)
meaning: Polite non-past negative form of a verb.

title: ～たい (want to)
meaning: Expresses the speaker's desire to do something.

examples.reading: にほんへいきたいです。
examples.en: I want to go to Japan.

title: ～ましょう (let's)
meaning: Invites or suggests doing something together.

examples.reading: いっしょにたべましょう。

title: から (from / because)
meaning: Indicates a starting point or a reason.

examples.reading: くじからはたらきます。

title: まで (until / to)
meaning: Indicates an end point in time or space.

examples.reading: ごじまでべんきょうします。

title: と (and / with)
meaning: Joins nouns exhaustively, or means 'together with'.

examples.reading: ともだちとはなします。
